scope: Visible Android route-backed lifecycle breadth: Moneyline Book plus Spread bid/Sell open/cancel lifecycle
eventIdentity: EL-A Provider Breadth World Cup Live

assertions.backendHealth: Backend /api/health was required before launch; the proof aborts instead of falling back when unavailable.
assertions.moneylineBreadth: Initial Book baseline is route-backed Moneyline with selected-family-Moneyline selected-market-type-winner selected-line-none and orderbook-source-orderbook-route/orderbook-status-ready.
assertions.spreadSelection: Grouped selector switches to provider-backed Spread 1.5 without losing selected market/outcome/line/period/provider identity.
assertions.stagedBid: Tapping order-book-bid-level-fa8cde2d-acf8-4f8a-89d5-710203200c8f-1 stages Sell bid at 0.50 USDT / 50c.
assertions.ticketLimit: Sell ticket exposes ticket-limit-side-bid ticket-limit-price-50 ticket-limit-decimal-0.50 and submits at ticket-price-line 50c.
assertions.latestOrder: Latest order card exposes order-status-open and the same sell-side portfolio limit/source/market/outcome/line/period/provider tokens.
assertions.openOrder: Open order row shows Limit 50% and the same order-time snapshot identity.
assertions.activityHistory: Sell filled and canceled activity cards retain portfolio-limit-side-bid portfolio-limit-price-50 and the same selected route-backed Book identity.
assertions.fallbackGuard: Portfolio and activity reject Mexico fixture labels, Team to Advance, and generic Match Winner fallback labels for the selected spread Sell lifecycle.

remainingGaps: This visible proof uses mock trading with server market-data mode; it proves visible route-backed identity preservation, not production order execution.
Totals lifecycle breadth and immutable first-class backend selection snapshots remain future hardening.
